prompt: Write a Python script using MDAnalysis and matplotlib that analyses a GROMACS or OpenMM molecular dynamics trajectory (≥1 µs) of human β2-adrenergic receptor in a POPC lipid bilayer (PDB 2RH1). The script should: (1) compute per-residue RMSF over the last 500 ns of trajectory, (2) extract helix tilt angles for TM3, TM5, TM6 using principal axis calculation, (3) compute the TM3/TM6 distance as a proxy for receptor activation state, (4) calculate lipid order parameters (SCD) for the surrounding POPC bilayer, (5) produce: RMSF heatmap on structure using NGLView, TM3/TM6 distance time series, and lipid order parameter bar chart. Include Jupyter notebook and standalone script versions, with environment.yml.
source_citation: Derived from RCSB PDB (2RH1) and MD simulation literature; target: β2AR; organism: Homo sapiens; catalogue version v1.0

_provenance.target_class: G protein-coupled receptor (GPCR, class A seven-transmembrane receptor)
